rulings.Case Title: KAREN LUM VS. CARLOS M. ARROYO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mntn And Mtn To Reinstate Order Of Jan 14, 20
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 13, 2020 line 2. DEFENDANT CARLOS ARROYO, SHARLYN ARROYO Ntc Of Mntn And Mtn To Reinstate Order Of Jan 14, 20 READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Motion to Reinstate Order of January 14, 2020 is GRANTED as follows: (1) October 24, 2020 default is set aside. Answer to be filed and served no later than July 13, 2020 by noon; (2) Defense counsel to prepare order and provide it to the Court in accordance with the instructions provided above. If a complaint order is not timely presented or an answer is not timely filed, this ruling shall not be effective and Plaintiff may proceed with the action as if default is in effect. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SWORDS TO PLOWSHARES VS. GEORGE TRINIDAD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 13, 2020 line 3. PLAINTIFF SWORDS TO PLOWSHARES MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment is GRANTED. Plaintiff shifted its burden, even if all of Defendant's written evidentiary objections were sustained. Defendant failed to create a triable issue of fact with competent admissible evidence. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: EMESE TIVADAR VS. JAMIE AQUILAR ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 13, 2020 line 4. DEFENDANT MELBA AMBROCIO, CRISTIAN MACARIO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendants Christian Macario and Melba Ambrocio's Demurrer to Plaintiff's Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Notice to Pay Rent of Quit attached to the Complaint and incorporated thereto was directed to and demanded rent payment from "unauthorized sub-tenants and tenants in possession" effectively authorizing occupancy/creating tenancy where none may have existed before. Exhibits take precedence over potentially conflicting allegations in the complaint. =(501/CFH)
